skill.name: expert-interview-topics
skill.description: Generates professional interview titles and questions based on expert background and topic. Provides a structured workflow for interview preparation.
skill.summary: Creates structured interview titles and questions from expert background and discussion topics.
skill.author: AIPOCH

security_audit.summary: All 46 static analysis findings are false positives after manual evaluation. The 16 'Weak cryptographic algorithm' findings flag argparse declarations, audit result JSON text (containing words like 'modest deduction'), and SKILL.md descriptions - no cryptographic operations exist in the code. The 28 'Ruby/shell backtick execution' findings are markdown ```bash code block delimiters in SKILL.md showing documentation examples for users. The 2 'Hardcoded URL' and 1 'System reconnaissance' findings are standard source attribution and documentation about output quality. This skill is a legitimate Python script that generates interview question prompts and poses no security risk.

high_findings.title: Weak Cryptographic Algorithm (False Positive)
high_findings.description: All 16 instances flagged by the static analyzer contain no cryptographic code. In scripts/main.py:48 and scripts/validate_skill.py:12, the pattern matches argparse.ArgumentParser declarations. In expert-interview-topics_audit_result_v2.json (lines 67, 72, 97, 132, 167, 237), matches occur in text like 'modest deduction remained in security' and similar evaluation notes. In SKILL.md (lines 3, 17, 19, 60, 104, 117), matches occur in descriptive markdown text about the skill's purpose. No MD5, SHA, AES, RSA, or any other cryptographic function exists in this codebase.

medium_findings.title: Shell Backtick Execution Patterns (False Positive)
medium_findings.description: All 28 instances flagged as 'Ruby/shell backtick execution' in SKILL.md are markdown code block delimiters (triple backticks with language identifier ```bash) containing bash command examples for user documentation. The flagged content includes commands like 'python scripts/main.py --help' and 'python scripts/main.py --topic "AI" --background "Dr. Smith"' shown as user-facing usage examples. No shell execution occurs from the skill itself. The triple-backtick syntax is standard GitHub-Flavored Markdown for rendering code blocks.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded URL in Documentation (False Positive)
low_findings.description: Static analyzer flagged a GitHub URL at SKILL.md:7. This is a source attribution link formatted as '> **Source**: [https://github.com/aipoch/medical-research-skills](...)'. It is a standard markdown reference link and not a runtime network request. The skill makes no HTTP connections or API calls during execution.

low_findings.title: System Reconnaissance Reference (False Positive)
low_findings.description: Static analyzer flagged SKILL.md:48 as 'system reconnaissance'. The line reads: 'Output discipline: keep results reproducible, identify assumptions explicitly, and avoid undocumented side effects.' This is a quality assurance instruction in the skill documentation, describing expected output practices. It does not perform any system information gathering or reconnaissance.

content.user_title: Create Expert Interview Questions and Titles
content.value_statement: Preparing structured expert interviews takes significant time and effort. This skill generates professional interview titles and questions from expert background and topic information.
actual_capabilities: Generates interview title suggestions from expert background and interview topic
Selects the most relevant interview title from a set of generated options
Creates 4 to 6 structured interview questions aligned with the selected title
Reads existing interview transcripts to inform question generation
Outputs reusable prompt templates for each step of the interview workflow
limitations: Does not conduct live interviews or interact with human participants
Generated questions require human review and customization before use
Cannot access external databases or verify factual claims
Output quality depends on completeness of provided background information

use_cases.title: Journalist Interview Preparation
use_cases.description: Journalists preparing for expert interviews can generate professional titles and questions tailored to the guest background and discussion topic.

use_cases.title: Academic Research Interviews
use_cases.description: Researchers planning academic interviews can quickly create structured question sets aligned with research topics and expert profiles.

use_cases.title: Podcast Content Development
use_cases.description: Content creators developing podcast episodes can generate interview frameworks and discussion questions from guest background information.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Interview Title Generation
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the topic and expert background to generate three professional interview titles. Include the expert name and title in each option. Make titles appealing to a general audience.
prompt_templates.scenario: Generating initial title options for a new expert interview

prompt_templates.title: Interview Title Selection
prompt_templates.prompt: Review the generated interview titles and select the most relevant and attractive option. Output only the selected title text without additional commentary.
prompt_templates.scenario: Choosing the best title from generated options

prompt_templates.title: Question Generation with Transcript
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate 4 to 6 interview questions based on the selected title, interview topic, and reference transcript. Ensure questions use objective phrasing and align with the discussion direction.
prompt_templates.scenario: Creating detailed questions informed by an existing transcript

prompt_templates.title: Complete Interview Workflow
prompt_templates.prompt: Run the full three-step workflow: first generate interview titles, then select the best title, and finally create structured interview questions. Follow each step in sequence and use outputs from previous steps as inputs.
prompt_templates.scenario: End-to-end interview preparation from topic to question set

output_examples.input: Topic: AI in Healthcare, Background: Dr. Sarah Chen, Chief Scientist at HealthAI Labs
output_examples.output: The skill generated three title options: 'Transforming Medicine: Dr. Sarah Chen on the Future of AI in Healthcare', 'Beyond the Hype: A Conversation with Dr. Sarah Chen on Real-World AI Applications', and 'Healing with Algorithms: Dr. Sarah Chen Explores AI's Role in Modern Medicine'. The most relevant title was selected and used to create 5 interview questions covering clinical adoption, data privacy, regulatory challenges, and future directions.

output_examples.input: Topic: Climate Policy, Background: Prof. James Wilson, Lead Author at IPCC
output_examples.output: The skill generated three title options focused on climate policy and research impact. After selection, 6 interview questions were produced covering recent IPCC findings, policy implementation challenges, scientific consensus, and recommended actions for policymakers.

best_practices: Provide detailed expert background including name, title, organization, and research areas for better title generation
Review and customize generated questions for your specific interview context and audience
Use the file option to include relevant transcripts for more informed and targeted questions
anti_patterns: Skipping expert background details to save time results in generic and less relevant titles
Using the skill without specifying a clear interview topic produces unfocused questions
Accepting generated questions without human review may include inaccuracies or irrelevant content

faq.question: What does this skill do?
faq.answer: It generates professional interview titles and questions based on an expert background and a discussion topic. It follows a three-step workflow: title generation, title selection, and question creation.

faq.question: What information do I need to provide?
faq.answer: You need to provide the interview topic and detailed expert background. An optional transcript file can be included for more informed questions.

faq.question: Can I use existing interview transcripts?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill accepts an optional transcript file path. It reads the transcript and uses it as reference context for generating more targeted questions.

faq.question: How many questions does it generate?
faq.answer: The skill generates 4 to 6 interview questions. The exact number depends on the topic complexity and the information provided in the background.

faq.question: Can I use it for any subject or domain?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill is domain-agnostic and works with any subject matter. The output quality depends on the completeness of the expert background and topic details you provide.

faq.question: How should I use the generated output?
faq.answer: The skill outputs prompt templates. You should review and customize the generated content for your specific interview context before use.
